name: Todoist MCP Server
what_it_does: Todoist MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Todoist's task management app — creating and updating tasks, managing projects and sections, querying task lists and due dates, handling labels and filters, and integrating Todoist's personal productivity system into agent-driven task automation workflows.
use_cases: Creating tasks and projects from personal productivity agents
Querying today's tasks and upcoming deadlines from planning agents
Organizing tasks into projects and sections from organization agents
Completing and rescheduling tasks from personal assistant agents
Managing labels and filters for task organization from workflow agents
Syncing action items from meetings into Todoist from capture agents
not_for: Team project management at scale (ClickUp, Asana, or Jira better suited)
Complex workflow automation with dependencies (use dedicated workflow tools)
best_when: An agent needs to manage personal or team tasks in Todoist — for personal productivity automation, quick task capture, or daily planning.
avoid_when: You need complex project management with Gantt charts, resource management, or cross-team visibility.

auth.notes: Todoist API token (personal, full access) or OAuth 2.0 for user-facing apps. API token provides access to all tasks and projects. No scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 5 active projects, up to 5 collaborators, basic features
pricing.notes: Affordable personal productivity tool. API access included. Free tier suitable for individual use. MCP server is community open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit at 450 req/15min — sufficient for most agent use cases
Task due dates use RFC 3339 format — agents must format dates correctly
Project IDs are integers — don't confuse with project names
Todoist uses a sync API and REST API — this MCP likely uses REST v2
Community MCP server — may not expose all Todoist features (filters, reminders)
Recurring tasks create new task instances — understanding recurrence behavior important
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API token with full account access — no scopes. OAuth 2.0 for user-facing apps. GDPR. Community MCP server — review before production.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API token — full account access, no scopes. OAuth 2.0 available. GDPR. Community MCP server.
